Article Title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap: KL Rahul overtakes Abhishek Sharma to reclaim top spot; Sanju Samson jumps to fifth
Article Text:
KL Rahul (Pic credit: IPL)

Watch Venugopal Rao explains the key decisions which helped DC beat RCB Venugopal Rao explains the key decisions which helped DC beat RCB

Poll Can KL Rahul maintain his Orange Cap lead in IPL 2026? Yes, he will stay on top No, someone else will take over

IPL 2026 Orange Cap standings after DC vs CSK match:

Rank Player Runs Matches Innings SR Ave. HS 1 KL Rahul (DC) 445 10 10 180.89 49.44 152* 2 Abhishek Sharma (SRH) 440 10 10 206.57 48.89 135* 3 Heinrich Klaasen (SRH) 425 10 10 156.82 53.13 65* 4 Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (RR) 404 10 10 237.64 40.40 103 5 Sanju Samson (CSK) 402 10 10 167.50 57.43 115* 6 Sai Sudharsan (GT) 385 10 10 158.43 38.50 100 7 Ryan Rickelton (MI) 380 8 8 190.95 54.29 123* 8 Virat Kohli (RCB) 379 9 9 165.50 54.14 81 9 Shubman Gill (GT) 378 9 9 154.91 42.00 86 10 Prabhsimran Singh (PBKS) 361 9 8 174.39 51.57 80*

NEW DELHI: Delhi Capitals opener KL Rahul will be disappointed with his and his team’s performance against Chennai Super Kings at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on Tuesday, as they suffered an eight-wicket drubbing at home. Rahul contributed just 12 off 13 balls for the home side, but those runs were enough for him to reclaim the top spot in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap race Rahul leapfrogged Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Abhishek Sharma to wear the Orange Cap again. He now has 445 runs from 10 games at an average of 49.44 and a strike rate of 180.89. He has scored one hundred and three fifties so far in 10 innings.SRH opener Abhishek is just five runs behind Rahul in second, with 440 runs from 10 games. He has scored his runs at an average of 48.89 and a strike rate of 206.57, including one hundred and three fifties.Abhishek’s teammate Heinrich Klaasen is third in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap race with 425 runs from 10 games. He has scored those runs at an average of 53.13 and a strike rate of 156.82, with four half-centuries.Rajasthan Royals teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is next on the list in fourth, with 404 runs from 10 games. The youngster has scored his runs at a stunning strike rate of 237.64 this season, including one hundred and two fifties.CSK opener Sanju Samson , who won the Player of the Match against DC, scored a match-winning 87 not out off 52 balls to take his season tally to 402 runs from 10 games. Samson jumped to fifth in the Orange Cap standings with this match-winning effort. The CSK batter has scored at an average of 57.43 and a strike rate of 167.50, and has two hundreds and one fifty this season.
Article Summary: KL Rahul reclaims top spot in IPL 2026 Orange Cap race. Rahul scored 445 runs from 10 games at an average of 49.44 and a strike rate of 180.89. Rahul leapfrogged Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Abhishek Sharma to wear the Orange Cap.

Article Title: Bjp Elections Victory: SC, ST voters played pivotal role in BJP’s Bengal and Assam victories
Article Text:
.





NEW DELHI: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes are not seen by BJP ’s rivals as its most avid supporters but it is these disadvantaged communities which played a key role in its landslide victories in West Bengal and Assam, as it has reaped even a bigger harvest in reserved seats in the two states than its overall impressive strike rate.In Bengal, BJP bagged all 16 seats reserved for STs, primarily in north Bengal and Junglemahal, the two regions that gave the party its first foothold in the state and have now become its citadel.In 68 constituencies reserved for SCs, BJP won 51, exactly 75%, reducing TMC to 57 as Dalits, which include Matuas, rallied behind the party like never before.If BJP’s surge to 207 seats in the 294-member assembly (one seat will go to repoll) from 77 in 2021 marks a huge broadening of support, its dominant show in reserved seats stands out for the deep roots it has made among these communities.That Matuas despite their misgivings about SIR and TMC’s intense attempts to woo them, stayed solidly with BJP highlights their trust in the party, its functionaries said.In Assam, things went even better for the party. With BJP stitching a broad alliance to appeal to different ethnic groups in a state full of diverse identities, which have been at times a source of fracture, it succeeded in annihilating its main rival Congress.BJP won five and its allies three of nine seats reserved for SCs, with Congress getting one. In 19 seats allocated to STs, BJP won 13 and its two allies BPF and AGO the rest as Congress drew a blank.
Article Summary: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes played a key role in landslide victories in West Bengal and Assam. BJP bagged all 16 seats reserved for STs, primarily in north Bengal and Junglemahal. In 68 constituencies reserved for SCs, BJP won 51, exactly 75%, reducing TMC to 57.

NEW DELHI: Tamil Nadu appears to be witnessing a dramatic political churn as counting trends from the 2026 Assembly elections point towards a breakthrough performance by the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, led by actor-turned-politician Joseph Vijay . Early leads across multiple constituencies suggest that the party, making its electoral debut, is not just competing but potentially reshaping the state’s long-standing Dravidian political order. From Thanjavur to Salem and parts of Chennai, TVK candidates have taken significant leads, signalling a shift in voter sentiment.The ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, led by Chief Minister M. K. Stalin, appears to be facing unexpected setbacks. In key constituencies such as Kolathur, Stalin himself has trailed in early rounds against TVK’s V S Babu, while senior leaders like Anbil Mahesh have also found themselves in tight contests. Although the DMK has managed to hold leads in pockets like Valparai and Madurai East, the broader trend indicates a challenging outing for the incumbent party, raising questions about the durability of its governance plank.Meanwhile, the All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam and other regional players such as the Desiya Murpokku Dravida Kazhagam and Pattali Makkal Katchi have shown mixed performances. While AIADMK leaders like Edappadi K. Palaniswami have secured early leads in select constituencies, the party appears to be trailing behind TVK’s surge. DMDK and PMK have managed to stay competitive in certain seats, indicating a fragmented contest beyond the top two players.Across the state, TVK’s performance has been particularly striking. Candidates such as S Vijay Saravanan in Thanjavur, Sukumar in Sulur, and Vignesh in Kinathukadavu have established comfortable leads, while the party has also gained ground in urban centres like Chennai and Salem. Celebrations among party workers, marked by chants and symbolic displays, reflect growing confidence that the party could emerge as a major force, if not form the government outright. Article Title: Badruddin Ajmal: Badruddin Ajmal’s perfume loses fragrance
Article Text:
Watch Assam Polls Get Twist As BJP Fields Himanta From Jalukbari, New Entrant Bordoloi From Dispur Seat Assam Polls Get Twist As BJP Fields Himanta From Jalukbari, New Entrant Bordoloi From Dispur Seat

GUWAHATI: The scent of success wore off. Badruddin Ajmal’s AIUDF, once the principal voice of Muslim minorities in Assam, has crashed from 16 seats in the assembly to just two, signalling a sharp erosion of its political relevance as BJP ’s wider sweep and Congress ’ consolidation redraw the map.The 70-year-old businessman-politician, whose family-run fragrance house, Ajmal Perfumes, is a global brand, returned to the fray after his 2024 LS defeat in Dhubri. He won from Binnakandi in Hojai. Mazibur Rahman held Dalgaon. Elsewhere, the party collapsed across lower Assam and Barak Valley despite over 90% turnout in Muslim-majority constituencies.The surge in participation did not translate into votes for AIUDF. Instead, it consolidated behind Congress, which swept most minority-heavy seats. Constituencies with over 95% Muslim electorates — including Dhubri, Gauripur, Mankachar, Jaleswar, Chenga and Samaguri — tilted decisively toward Congress.Political analyst Hafiz Rashid Ahmed Choudhury attributed the slide to leadership choices and perception. “Ajmal could have projected someone else. A man who cannot walk properly became a candidate.It signals they want to keep the party confined to family,” he said.AIUDF functionaries pointed to “wrong candidate selection” and a direct contest with Congress — unlike their 2021 alliance — as key factors. Rahman, who retained Dalgaon, framed the verdict as a broader opposition defeat. “We thought Gaurav Gogoi would become the CM, but he lost. It’s painful,” he said, crediting his own win to local development.Formed in 2005 after SC scrapped IMDT Act, it had built its base among Bengali-speaking Muslims. Now, with no MPs and two MLAs, its future looks uncertain.
Article Summary: Badruddin Ajmal’s AIUDF, once the principal voice of Muslim minorities in Assam, has crashed from 16 seats in the assembly to just two. The party collapsed across lower Assam and Barak Valley despite over 90% turnout in Muslim-majority constituencies.

Article Title: Assembly elections result: BJP is Bengal Janata’s Party, Vijay Divas in Tamil Nadu, Keralove for Congress
Article Text:
.

Change reaction in 3 states; Assam keeps Biswas in Himanta

.

Watch ‘Ram Rajya Arrived In Bengal’: BJP Workers Celebrate As Party Holds Majority Lead Over TMC ‘Ram Rajya Arrived In Bengal’: BJP Workers Celebrate As Party Holds Majority Lead Over TMC

Victory in Bengal signals BJP’s reach beyond Hindi heartland

.

.

.

.

It was a day of dramatic upheavals. BJP finally managed to win the state that had virtually become its final frontier over the past 10 years, Tamil superstar Vijay made a stunning political debut and looks set to be the next chief minister of Tamil Nadu, while Congress got the better of CPM in Kerala, clearing the way for a Left-mukt Bharat.Amid the anti-incumbency wave, Himanta Biswa Sharma held firm, routing Congress to take BJP to the party’s first-ever majority of its own in Assam. The victory of NDA in tiny Puducherry may not be in the same league, but served to drive home the message that smart politics and good alliances can help incumbents entrench themselves. The political significance of BJP’s victory is enormous.It owes hugely to the continuing popularity of PM Narendra Modi, despite being at the helm for 12 years — a story of remarkable resilience in an era of ever-soaring expectations. It reinforces the message about the party’s countrywide dominance and, along with DMK’s loss in Tamil Nadu, will trigger an upheaval in INDIA bloc ranks, making the LS an easier place for the Modi govt.Congress has already begun warming up to Vijay at the cost of its “ideological brotherhood” with DMK. BJP is now in control of the entire east, with the exception of Jharkhand, where it is the principal opposition and can realistically hope for success in the 2029 state polls.A BJP-led Bengal can smoothen matters for the Centre on issues as diverse as GST and completion of border fencing. It will force “secular” players to take a fresh look at their playbook as they seek to negotiate the tension between expectations of large sections who are buying into Hindutva themes, and the need to cater to demands of Muslims. The presence of a sizeable Muslim population alone does not provide safe sanctuary from advancing Hindutva sentiments.The import of the battle of WB transcends politics. It also signals the possibility of the theme of co- existing with regional distinctiveness, without compromising on its core. It also shows that the Hindi heartland roots of BJP are no longer the fetters they once were.Even in Kerala, BJP managed to win three seats, from none earlier. It is also a reminder that Modi, with his promise of development, national glory and “tough on security” credentials, can break through the resistance of satraps. Vijay also shattered the assumptions that had, because of their endurance, turned in- to certitudes — like the Dravidian duopoly where office had rotated between DMK and AIADMK over last five decades.His senior and “superstar” Rajinikanth, who had potential to challenge the establishment and was urged to take the plunge by his supporters, failed to summon courage. Vijay dared to dream big and has been rewarded for that. Awave of disgust with corruption and rising crimes against Dalits and women un- der DMK has brought him to the gates of Fort St George.DMK tried using a crude anti- Hindi and anti-Sanatan plank. It did not work, with a large number of Gen Z voters opting for the new promise: Vijay who, combining freshness with time-tested tricks like mangalsutras for women, managed to win over women while also peeling away the support of DMK among Dalits, Christians and Muslims.His resultant emergence as a stronger vehicle of anti- DMK sentiments seemed to have also helped attract votes that would have otherwise gone to AIADMK. He is on the verge of becoming the first Christian CM of Tamil Nadu, (the fourth one in the south after A K Antony, Y S Rajasekhara Reddy and Jagan Reddy).The loosening of the duopoly’s grip of the state can potentially create opportunities for diverse players: from Seeman to Congress and BJP. Vijay has, in any case, refrained from taking a hardline stand against the north and Sanatanis, and this opens up the possibility of better working ties between Centre and Chennai.Victory for Congress-led coalition in Kerala comes as a consolation trophy for Congress: something the party desperately needed to make a statement of its presence. But the success carries with itself the challenge of dealing with the perception of rising Hindu sentiment at a time when its dependence on the Indian Union Muslim League and Christian outfit Kerala Congress (Joseph) has grown.Of course, it also has to sort out the leadership issue, with V D Satheesan unlikely to give up his claim in favour of party brass’s favourite — K C Venugopal — who is not an MLA. For CPM, defeat marks not just loss of the only state where they held office, but further erosion as an electoral force. BJP’s victory in Assam was not in doubt.But its scale came as a surprise. The tally achieved despite decade-long incumbency and a determined effort for higher Muslim consolidation which saw Deobandi outfit Jamat-i-Ule-ma abandoning their own Badruddin Ajmal of AIUDF, showed smart footwork by CM Himanta Sarma who opposed Bangla-speaking Muslims, dubbing them Bangladeshi in- filtrators.He managed to rally non-Muslims, and his success has exposed Congress to the risk of appearing a pro-Muslim outfit; a vulnerability which may get enhanced because of its alliance with IUML in Kerala as well as the thinning support base among Hindus in different parts.
Article Summary: BJP finally managed to win the state that had virtually become its final frontier over the past 10 years. Tamil superstar Vijay made a stunning political debut and looks set to be the next chief minister of Tamil Nadu. Congress got the better of CPM in Kerala, clearing the way for a Left-mukt Bharat.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumor: Minnesota Vikings could target former $5M Baltimore Ravens WR to address offensive gaps after tough season
Article Text:
Minnesota Vikings could target former $5M Baltimore Ravens WR to address offensive gaps after tough season.(Image via X)

Minnesota Vikings urged to make a move for the 35 year old popular former Baltimore Ravens ’ wide receiver , DeAndre Hopkins



The Minnesota Vikings have had a pretty brutal run on the field in the last season after a pretty impressive performance in the 2024 season.With 14 wins and 3 losses in the 2024 season, the Minnesota Vikings ended up recording just 9 wins and 8 losses in the previous season. Ahead of the new season, the team remains focused on fixing the roster.A few hours ago, CBS Sports’ Jordan Dajani predicted that there is a chance that the Minnesota Vikings could land the veteran wide receiver and former Baltimore Ravens star player, DeAndre Hopkins.CBS Sports’ Jordan Dajani noted in a recent report, “The Minnesota Vikings have a new quarterback in Kyler Murray , whom Hopkins is very familiar with. The two players spent three seasons together with the Arizona Cardinals from 2020-2022.”CBS Sports’ Jordan Dajani added, “Hopkins' first season in Arizona was one of his best, as he caught 115 passes for 1,407 yards and six touchdowns.”CBS Sports’ Jordan Dajani continued, “The Vikings have a talented wide receiving room headlined by Justin Jefferson and Jordan Addison, but adding a WR3 like Hopkins who has established chemistry with Murray would be ideal.”However, the Minnesota Vikings have yet to react to the trade talks concerning the former Baltimore Ravens’ star player, DeAndre Hopkins.The 35 year old wide receiver DeAndre Hopkins has had an interesting transitional phase in the last few seasons.While in the 2024 season, DeAndre Hopkins started with the Tennessee Titans and recorded 15 passes for 173 yards and a touchdown across six games, he was traded to the Kansas City Chiefs in the middle of the season.The popular wide receiver transitioned to a secondary role with the Chiefs and finished the season with 56 receptions, 610 yards, and 5 touchdowns in 16 games.In the last season, DeAndre Hopkins signed a one year deal with the Baltimore Ravens as his performance recorded a slight decline.DeAndre Hopkins finished the 2025 season with 22 receptions, 330 yards, 2 touchdowns in 17 games.For now, all eyes are on where DeAndre Hopkins ends up next as many feel he might go back to the Baltimore Ravens.
Article Summary: Minnesota Vikings urged to make a move for the 35 year old popular former Baltimore Ravens ’ wide receiver, DeAndre Hopkins. The Vikings have had a pretty brutal run on the field in the last season after a pretty impressive performance in the 2024 season. The Minnesota Vikings have yet to react to the trade talks concerning the former Ravens’ star player, DeAndreHopkins.

Article Title: What happened to Kyle Loftis? Cause of death questions grow after 1320Video founder dies months after major crash
Article Text:
What happened to Kyle Loftis? Cause of death questions grow after 1320Video founder dies months after major crash (Image via Getty)

Kyle Loftis built 1320Video into one of motorsports’ biggest online platforms

Kyle Loftis, the founder of popular motorsports platform 1320Video, died Tuesday night, leaving the car racing world shocked and heartbroken. The news was confirmed by the platform on social media, where the team shared an emotional message about the man who helped turn underground street racing videos into one of the biggest automotive communities online. “We are extremely saddened to share that Kyle Loftis, the founder of 1320video, passed away last night. We are in a state of shock,” the statement read. The team also said Kyle Loftis inspired millions of motorsports fans through his passion, creativity, and love for cars.Right now, an official cause of death has not been released. Many fans have been asking questions online because Loftis had survived a serious crash in December while filming content for the channel. However, there is still no confirmation that the earlier accident was connected to his death. The sudden news quickly spread across social media, with fans, racers, and creators sharing tributes and memories about the man many believe changed online car culture forever.Kyle Loftis launched 1320Video in 2003 with a simple idea, show real street cars, racers, and car culture in a raw and honest way. What started as a small project later became one of the most watched automotive platforms in the world.The channel now has nearly four million YouTube subscribers along with millions more followers across Instagram, Facebook, and other platforms.The company focused heavily on drag racing, street racing, dyno events, roll racing, car meets, and underground racing scenes from across the United States. The team regularly traveled around the world to film unique cars and racers that mainstream television rarely covered.Even though the platform often faced criticism because of its street racing content and the safety concerns linked to illegal racing, many fans still respected how authentic the videos felt. For years, 1320Video gave smaller racers and unknown car builders a chance to be seen by huge audiences online.Kyle Loftis also helped inspire a new generation of automotive creators. One of the biggest names connected to him was Garrett Mitchell, better known as Cleetus McFarland. Mitchell had recently gifted Loftis a Chevrolet Corvette ZR1 before the tragic news surfaced.Fans across social media continued posting emotional messages after the announcement. One tribute on X described Loftis as someone who made car culture feel “real, unfiltered, and truly for the community.” Others said his videos helped introduce them to racing and car building for the first time.For many people in motorsports, Kyle Loftis was not just a content creator. He was one of the early figures who helped move street racing videos from small forums and DVDs to mainstream internet entertainment watched by millions around the world.
Article Summary: Kyle Loftis, the founder of popular motorsports platform 1320Video, died Tuesday night. The news was confirmed by the platform on social media, where the team shared an emotional message about the man. The team also said Kyle Loftis inspired millions of motorsports fans through his passion, creativity, and love for cars. An official cause of death has not been released.

Article Title: IPL Points Table after Match 49: SRH climb to top spot; PBKS suffer three consecutive losses
Article Text:
Sunrisers Hyderabad (PTI Photo)

Watch James Franklin post-match PC: SRH surprised by pitch, hails Nitish-Klaasen rescue act James Franklin post-match PC: SRH surprised by pitch, hails Nitish-Klaasen rescue act

IPL Points Table after Match 49:

Pos Team P W L NR Pts NRR 1 SRH 11 7 4 0 14 +0.737 2 PBKS 10 6 3 1 13 +0.571 3 RCB 9 6 3 0 12 +1.420 4 RR 10 6 4 0 12 +0.510 5 GT 10 6 4 0 12 -0.147 6 CSK 10 5 5 0 10 +0.151 7 DC 10 4 6 0 8 -0.949 8 KKR 9 3 5 1 7 -0.539 9 MI 10 3 7 0 6 -0.649 10 LSG 9 2 7 0 4 -1.076

Sunrisers Hyderabad’s commanding 41-run win over Punjab Kings has shaken up the IPL 2026 points table, pushing them to the top in a tightly contested season.Riding on strong performances from Heinrich Klaasen and Ishan Kishan , SRH posted a daunting 235/4 before restricting PBKS to 202/7. The result not only handed Punjab their third straight loss but also saw them slip from the summit to second place..At the top, SRH now lead the standings with 14 points from 11 matches, winning seven and losing four, with a healthy net run rate of +0.737. Punjab Kings follow closely with 13 points from 10 games, including six wins, three losses and one no result, maintaining a strong NRR of +0.571 despite the defeat.Royal Challengers Bengaluru sit third with 12 points from nine matches and boast the best net run rate in the competition at +1.420. Rajasthan Royals and Gujarat Titans are level on 12 points as well, placed fourth and fifth respectively. RR have a slightly better NRR of +0.510 compared to GT’s -0.147, which could prove crucial in the playoff race.Chennai Super Kings remain in sixth position with 10 points from 10 games and an NRR of +0.151, still very much in the hunt. Delhi Capitals, in seventh, have eight points from 10 matches but their poor NRR of -0.949 continues to hurt their chances.Kolkata Knight Riders are eighth with seven points from nine games, including one no result, and an NRR of -0.539. Mumbai Indians are ninth with six points from 10 matches and an NRR of -0.649, needing a near-perfect finish to stay alive.At the bottom, Lucknow Super Giants have struggled the most, managing just two wins in nine matches. With four points and a net run rate of -1.076, their playoff hopes are hanging by a thread.
Article Summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Punjab Kings by 41 runs in IPL 2026 match. SRH now lead the standings with 14 points from 11 matches, winning seven and losing four. Punjab Kings follow closely with 13 points from 10 games, including six wins, three losses and one no result.

Article Title: Fever’s Caitlin Clark makes honest admission about facing Wings’ Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers in season opener
Article Text:
Caitlin Clark and Paige Bueckers. Image via: Brian Spurlock/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

Caitlin Clark addresses the upcoming season opener against the Wings



Caitlin Clark and Paige Bueckers. Image via: Brian Spurlock/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

Caitlin Clark on how injuries changed her perspective on the WNBA games



Caitlin Clark enters the new WNBA season carrying far more than excitement. After spending most of last year recovering from injuries and watching the postseason from the sidelines, the Indiana Fever guard finally gets her regular-season return Saturday against the Dallas Wings. The matchup already feels bigger than a normal opener, with two recent No. 1 picks, Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers, preparing to face the league’s brightest star in front of what is expected to be a packed Indiana crowd.Clark looked sharp during preseason action and Team USA appearances over the past few months, easing concerns about the injury that cut short her 2025 campaign. She scored 13 points in Indiana’s final preseason game against Nigeria and recently returned to full practice. Even the leg sleeve that sparked questions this week turned out to be nothing serious. Clark brushed it off with a smile, explaining it was simply “compression on the leg. ”The anticipation surrounding Saturday’s game goes beyond star power. It also reflects how quickly the WNBA is changing. Between Caitlin Clark, Paige Bueckers, and Azzi Fudd, the league suddenly has another generation of young faces carrying enormous expectations into nationally televised games.Indiana already saw Dallas during preseason play, although Clark exited early after a brief injury scare in a 15-point loss. She expects a different atmosphere this time with the regular season beginning at home and both teams bringing healthy rosters into the spotlight matchup.“It’s crazy. Actually, in that game, the last four No. 1 picks will be playing. So I don’t know how many times that’s ever happened,” Clark told Athlon Sports, addressing the highly anticipated preseason opener.“Obviously, with me and my teammate (Aliyah Boston), and then Azzi and Paige, so pretty cool dynamic, and I think it just speaks to the excitement of the players in our league. Obviously, the talent level that has been coming in, and I think it should be a really great game,” she added.Clark’s role inside Indiana’s offense may also look different this season. Coach Stephanie White wants to reduce some of the physical pressure opponents constantly place on the Fever star by using her more off the ball.Last season forced her to experience the game from a different angle while recovering, and she believes it changed how she leads teammates and understands the floor.“I think just like more than anything, leadership and experience,” Clark said.“Like this is my third year and even though I wasn’t out on the court last year a lot, you still learn on the sidelines. So I think that being somebody my teammates can rely on being a great communicator. Steph’s [Stephanie White] always coaching me on that area. I want to be able to communicate with my team, on the court, exactly what she [Coach White] wants and what I think is best for the team.”That adjustment comes after Clark carried one of the highest usage rates in the league during her rookie year while battling multiple lower-body injuries. Indiana believes balancing the offense more effectively could help preserve her health over a longer season.Clark also admitted she wants to impact games in smaller ways that often go unnoticed.“I try to be a great defensive rebounder. I need to improve. That’s something I didn’t do too much in the preseason games. But like I wan’t to get it…But you know, dead balls and things like that, it’s difficult to bring the ball 94 feet. But if my teammate is standing there not being guarded, why wouldn’t I just pass them the ball? That’s just unselfish play to make.”Clark is healthy again, and Indiana suddenly looks ready to push itself back into that conversation.
Article Summary: Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark returns to the court for the first time this season. The Fever face the Dallas Wings in their season opener on Saturday. The game will feature the last four No. 1 picks in the WNBA. Clark says she wants to improve as a communicator on the court.

Article Title: Hardik Pandya: IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya set to link up with Mumbai Indians in Raipur on Thursday; Suryakumar Yadav doubtful for RCB game
Article Text:
Hardik Pandya (ANI Photo)

Watch Vaibhav Sooryavanshi India debut: The calls are getting louder Vaibhav Sooryavanshi India debut: The calls are getting louder

Suryakumar Yadav doubtful for RCB game

NEW DELHI: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya is likely to join the squad in Raipur on Thursday for their fixture against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday. The all-rounder didn't travel with the first batch of players on Wednesday and will link up with the squad later.It is reliably learnt that Pandya was advised a precautionary rest for about a week after the back spasm he sustained against the Chennai Super Kings.This forced him to miss his side's home fixture against the Lucknow Super Giants and the decision on his participation in Raipur will likely be made closer to the fixture, just like the franchise did in the case of Rohit Sharma who missed nearly a month of competitive cricket due to hamstring injury.It is further understood that there is no need to panic. Rohit Sharma travelled with the first batch on Wednesday and will push to regain complete match fitness before the big fixture against the RCB.

Article Title: PBKS IPL 2026 playoff qualification scenarios: Punjab Kings still in control despite worrying slide
Article Text:
Punjab Kings' strong start is faltering with three consecutive losses, raising concerns about their playoff security. Sloppy fielding and a top-order batting collapse against Sunrisers Hyderabad highlighted recent struggles. While mathematically still in a good position, the team needs to quickly address these issues to avoid entering the playoffs with a losing momentum. Punjab remain in control of qualification mathematically. But unless they quickly clean up the details, they risk entering the playoffs - if they get there - carrying the form of a fading side rather than a genuine contender.

Punjab Kings IPL 2026 Points Table Standing

Matches: 10

Wins: 6

Losses: 3

No Result: 1

Points: 13

Net Run Rate: +0.571

Position: 2nd

Watch Vaibhav Sooryavanshi India debut: The calls are getting louder Vaibhav Sooryavanshi India debut: The calls are getting louder

IPL 2026 playoff qualification benchmark

14 points (7 wins): Minimum realistic cutoff

16 points (8 wins): Safe zone

What Punjab Kings need from here

Matches remaining: 4

Current points: 13

To reach 14 points: need 1 win from 4 matches

To reach 16 points: need 2 wins from 4 matches

Momentum slipping at the worst possible time

Upcoming IPL 2026 fixtures for Punjab Kings

May 11 vs Delhi Capitals (Dharamsala) - Must-win clash against a desperate side fighting elimination.

May 14 vs Mumbai Indians (Dharamsala) - Strong opportunity to regain momentum against an inconsistent opponent.

May 17 vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru (Dharamsala) - Potential top-two battle against one of the form teams of the season.

May 23 vs Lucknow Super Giants (Lucknow) - Final league game that could still impact playoff positioning.

PBKS’s IPL 2026 Playoff Qualification Scenarios

Win all 4 matches: Reach 21 points and likely secure top-two finish

Win 3 matches: Reach 19 points and comfortably qualify

Win 2 matches: Reach 17 points and likely qualify safely

Win only 1 match: Finish on 15 points and risk NRR complications

Lose all 4 matches: Remain on 13 points and likely face elimination

Poll What aspect should Punjab focus on to improve their chances? Strengthening batting order Bowling strategies and fielding

Punjab Kings suddenly look far less secure than they did a week ago. Their 33-run defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad in Hyderabad extended a worrying losing streak and is beginning to threaten their strong first half of the season. While Cooper Connolly’s unbeaten 107 provided late resistance, PBKS were outplayed across phases - sloppy catching, poor execution with the ball and another top-order collapse left them chasing the game throughout. After briefly sitting comfortably atop the table, Punjab are now entering the final stretch under genuine pressure.Punjab still remain inside the top two and retain a healthy Net Run Rate, but momentum has shifted sharply against them. Three successive defeats have dragged them back into the middle of a crowded playoff battle where a single poor week can dramatically alter standings. They are still well placed but not secure yet.The only major exception came in 2019, when Sunrisers Hyderabad qualified with 12 points — still the only instance of a team qualifying with fewer than 14 points and a team qualifying despite having more losses than wins.In most seasons, 7 wins keeps you alive, 8 wins puts you in control.Mathematically, Punjab remain in a comfortable position. Two wins from their last four matches should almost certainly seal qualification, while even one victory could keep them alive depending on results elsewhere.However, recent form has complicated what earlier looked straightforward. Three consecutive losses have increased pressure heading into a difficult run featuring direct playoff rivals like Royal Challengers Bengaluru and a desperate Delhi Capitals side fighting elimination.Punjab’s recent form paints the picture of a side losing control in small but significant moments. They have now lost three successive matches.Against SRH, the biggest damage came in the field. Multiple dropped catches allowed Ishan Kishan and Heinrich Klaasen to turn a strong start into a massive total. Kishan was dropped twice before making 55, while Klaasen survived on 9 and eventually scored 69.The batting has also become increasingly top-heavy. Once the top order failed against Hyderabad, PBKS spent most of the chase recovering rather than dictating terms. Cooper Connolly’s unbeaten hundred masked a broader issue: no other batter crossed 30 apart from Marcus Stoinis. Shreyas Iyer, Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh all fell early, forcing the middle order into unrealistic chase scenarios.Punjab Kings are still firmly in the playoff race, but the comfort around their position is beginning to disappear. The concern is not merely the losing streak. It is the manner of the defeats. Their fielding standards have dipped sharply, the batting has become overly dependent on recovery actsPunjab remain in control of qualification mathematically. But unless they quickly clean up the details, they risk entering the playoffs - if they get there - carrying the form of a fading side rather than a genuine contender.
Article Summary: Punjab Kings' strong start is faltering with three consecutive losses. Sloppy fielding and a top-order batting collapse highlighted recent struggles. Punjab remain in control of qualification mathematically. But unless they quickly clean up the details, they risk entering the playoffs carrying the form of a fading side rather than a genuine contender.

Article Title: Indian royalty at Met Gala: Gauravi Kumari brings Maharani Gayatri Devi’s elegance to red carpet, while Sawai Padmanabh Singh honours Jaipur legacy
Article Text:
(Image Credits: Instagram)

(Image Credits: Instagram)

Jaipur royals Sawai Padmanabh Singh and Gauravi Kumari at the Met Gala 2026

(Image Credits: Instagram)





Well, it's not every day you see an Indian prince and princess making their debut at the Met Gala fashion carpet, and this year’s heritage moment had it all. The event may be fashion’s biggest stage, but this year, it doubled as a royal revival as the Princess of Jaipur, Gauravi Kumari, made her debut, alongside the Maharaja of Jaipur, Sawai Padmanabh Singh.The sibling duo turned the carpet into a heritage runway, where one carried a vintage grace and the other brought the regality of a forgotten textile into a space that rarely looks beyond Western couture. Both their looks were developed by designer Prabal Gurung and looked nothing less than a royal dream.For the occasion, Princess Gauravi Kumari chose a beautiful saree from her grandmother Maharani Gayatri Devi’s closet. Drenched in pink hues with subtle self-sequins, the shade of pink carries its own familiarity and is tied to Jaipur as much as to her family. The saree has been reworked by Gurung into a gown while keeping the chiffon in order so it lies across the body with a swift ease, making one feel moved.She matched its prowess with beautiful pearls drawn from The Gem Palace in Jaipur, teamed with uncut diamonds and stunning rubies.The reference to wearing pearls and chiffon is closely associated with Maharani Gayatri Devi, who was known for this royal balance, and Gauravi’s Met Gala debut is a natural ode to her grandmother.Her brother, on the other hand, brought a Phulghar coat to light, which featured a deep midnight blue velvet base with a quilted cotton blend, aari and zardozi embroidery and was finished with dabka and resham grace. This statement royalty piece took over 600 hours of work and was also developed by Prabal Gurung.The whole idea of this was to bring the Rajasthani craftsmanship to the forefront, where the coat carries the elegance of a bandhgala and has an evenly spread weight that looks mesmerising. He paired this with black formal trousers that featured references to the city of Jaipur.'Pacho' as Padmanabh is fondly called went for a mirror sun motif, drawn from the Sri Niwas at the City Palace in Jaipur, which connects the entire garment to his Suryavanshi lineage. He completed the look with polki and jadau necklaces associated with Johri Bazaar in Jaipur, which sat perfectly with his Maharaja personality.Together, these two didn’t just attend the event but reframed the entire narrative. They proved that the Indian legacy, when worn right, can outshine even the most avant-garde spectacles and become the highlight of the hour. We loved the stylish Met Gala debut of this brother-sister duo, how about you?
Article Summary: The Princess of Jaipur, Gauravi Kumari, made her debut at the Met Gala. Sawai Padmanabh Singh made his debut alongside his sister. The sibling duo turned the carpet into a heritage runway. Both their looks were developed by designer Prabal Gurung.
